name: ntfy-blade-mcp
title: ntfy Notifications
description: ntfy push notification MCP — publish, poll, topic reservation, and token management. Double-gate security with PII scrubbing.
tagline: Push notifications with priority, scheduling, and PII scrubbing
author: groupthink-dev

env.name: NTFY_BASE_URL
env.description: ntfy server URL (e.g. https://ntfy.sh or self-hosted)

env.name: NTFY_TOKEN
env.description: Bearer token (tk_...). Optional for public topics

env.name: NTFY_DEFAULT_TOPIC
env.description: Default topic when none specified in publish

env.name: NTFY_WRITE_ENABLED
env.description: Enable write operations (publish, cancel, reserve, tokens)

setup.help_text: Create an access token in your ntfy server → Account → Access tokens. For self-hosted servers, use the admin credentials.
setup.blurb: Push notifications via ntfy — publish with priority, tags, markdown, actions, and scheduled delivery. Double-gate security: env gate + per-call confirmation.

highlights.text: Publish — push messages with priority, tags, and markdown
Scheduling — delayed delivery with at/in syntax
Actions — attach view, broadcast, and HTTP action buttons
Double-gate — env gate plus per-call confirmation on writes
PII scrubbing — automatic redaction of sensitive data
Self-hosted or ntfy.sh — works with any ntfy server

readme:
### What it does

ntfy Blade MCP gives your AI agent the ability to send push notifications to any device subscribed to an ntfy topic. It supports rich messages with priority levels, emoji tags, markdown formatting, click actions, and scheduled delivery.

Your agent can send alerts, poll for incoming messages on a topic, manage topic reservations, and handle access tokens — all through a single MCP server.

### Token efficiency

ntfy's API is already lightweight, but the MCP adds concise formatting for poll results and topic listings, stripping internal metadata and returning only actionable fields.

### Safety model

Publishing notifications is a visible action (devices will buzz), so all write operations are double-gated:

- **Environment gate** — `NTFY_WRITE_ENABLED` must be `true` to unlock publish, cancel, reserve, and token operations
- **Confirm gate** — each write tool requires `confirm: true` per call
- **PII scrubbing** — automatic redaction of email addresses, phone numbers, and other sensitive patterns before messages leave the MCP

Read operations (polling topics, listing reservations) work immediately with no gates.

### Compatibility

Works with the public ntfy.sh service or any self-hosted ntfy server. Bearer token authentication is optional for public topics but recommended for private ones.

scenarios.label: Send a priority alert
scenarios.body: Publish a high-priority notification with emoji tags and a click-through URL to a specific ntfy topic. The message is PII-scrubbed before delivery.

scenarios.label: Schedule a reminder notification
scenarios.body: Compose a notification with scheduled delivery for a future time, attach action buttons for snooze or dismiss, and verify the message appears in the topic's pending queue.

scenarios.label: Poll and respond to incoming messages
scenarios.body: Poll a topic for recent messages, filter by priority or tag, and take action on each message — forwarding high-priority items or acknowledging routine ones.
